overview: Lorehold is a spell-slinging engine that converts instant and sorcery density into Treasure tokens and mana acceleration. It aims to out-value opponents by casting a high volume of cheap spells, leveraging Treasure generation for card advantage, mana flexibility, and late-game finishers, while controlling the board with efficient R/W removal and counters.
win_conditions: Hit the Mother Lode combined with a high-impact instant/sorcery like Lightning Helix, Swords to Plowshares, or a board wipe.
Treasure token chains can also fuel a win condition like a big artifact or direct damage spell.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands (preferably artifact lands like Lorehold Campus or mana rocks), Sol Ring/Mana Crypt, and at least one cheap instant/sorcery.
Prioritize hands that can cast a spell on turn 2 to trigger Lorehold's Treasure generation.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan if you have 4+ lands with no ramp/spells, or 3+ spells with no lands/mana acceleration.

early_game: Turn 1: Play a land, cast Sol Ring or another mana rock.
Turn 2: Cast a cheap spell to trigger Lorehold's Treasure generation, or play a Campus land.
Sequence mana rocks first to enable early spell casting.
If you have a Campus, consider tapping it for mana on turn 2 if you need it for a key spell, but remember sacrificing it is part of the deck's engine.
mid_game: Balance land drops with Lorehold's land-sacrifice mechanic.
Don't sacrifice lands you need for mana acceleration unless you're casting a high-impact spell.
Use Treasures for mana when you're mana-screwed, or for specific effects like casting more spells or activating abilities.
Keep hand size high by using Treasures for card draw or spell duplication.
Commit threats when you have interaction ready.
Use Lorehold's Treasure generation to fuel multiple spells per turn.
Hold back if you're low on removal and facing a board-stomper.
Prioritize clearing key permanents over casting filler spells.
late_game: Hit the Mother Lode combined with a high-impact instant/sorcery like Lightning Helix, Swords to Plowshares, or a board wipe.
Treasure token chains can also fuel a win condition like a big artifact or direct damage spell.
Go all-in when you have 3+ Treasures, a cleared board, or when the opponent is at lethal range.
Commit when you can cast multiple spells in response to their interaction.

sequencing_tips: Common mistake: Sacrificing too many lands too early, leaving you mana-flooded or unable to cast spells.
Always calculate if the Treasure generation is worth the land loss.
Synergize Treasures with cards that care about artifacts or treasure tokens.
Watch out for land destruction in a deck that relies on land sacrifice.
Prioritize casting spells over hoarding Treasures unless a specific finisher requires them.

matchups.advice: Use cheap, uncounterable threats if available, or overpay with Treasures to cast spells. Use instant-speed interaction to clear their counterspellers. Consider spell-skew or redirect effects to bypass counters.
Apply early pressure with cheap spells and Treasures. Use artifact destruction and land destruction to slow them down. Don't trade resources; focus on board wipes and targeted removal.
Stabilize with efficient removal and board wipes. Use Treasures to cast multiple spells per turn to outpace their damage. Don't overcommit to a mana sink; prioritize survival.
Pressure with cheap, resilient threats. Use Treasures to cast spells around their removal. Force them to use resources on low-impact spells, then close the game with a finisher.

Win by having better resource management and more efficient spell sequencing. Use counterspells to protect your key spells. Prioritize clearing their Lorehold and generating more Treasures than they can.

common_threats: Watch for opponent's mana base and hand size.
If they're holding up blue mana, assume counterspells.
If they're tapping out for a big creature, have removal ready.
Track graveyard hate if they're playing reanimation.
Note when they start sacrificing lands for mana, as they might be playing a similar engine or ramp.
